appraisal_events.evidence: « poursuivant l'idée de la noblesse de comte de Forcheville... » ; « Je soigne en ce moment une baronne... Ils ont, en Poméranie, un lac... »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator frames Cottard as fixated on Forcheville's nobility and shows him boasting of aristocratic connections to ingratiate himself, which locally makes him look deferential and socially self-lowering.

status_effects.explanation: Cottard appears snobbish and obsequious by parading noble associations, which diminishes his local standing in the narrator's portrayal.
